issues.description: Cross-feature import: `products` feature imports directly from `cart` feature (`useAddToCart`). Both ProductCard.tsx and MobileBarWithCart.tsx follow the same pattern.
issues.rule: Features must not import directly from other features
issues.fix: This is a valid use of the cart's public barrel index (`@/features/cart`), so the import path is compliant. However, consider whether cart interaction belongs in the products presentation layer or should be composed at the page/provider level. The current pattern couples products to cart.

issues.description: Cross-feature imports in a page component: `products` feature imports from both `orders` and `seller-admins` features (`usePendingOrderCount`, `useDelegateCountsByProduct`, `SELLER_ADMINS_READ_PERMISSION`). This couples three features together in one component.
issues.rule: Features must not import directly from other features
issues.fix: Lift the data aggregation to the route-level (`app/` directory) or a shared hook, then pass the results as props to the feature pages. Alternatively, create a composition page in app/ that retrieves data from each feature independently.

issues.description: Cross-feature import: `seller-admins` feature imports `useProductById` from the `products` feature application layer.
issues.rule: Features must not import directly from other features
issues.fix: Pass `productId` and the resolved product data as props from the route wrapper, or expose a shared hook via `@/shared/`.

issues.description: Route page imports `PaymentMethodsPage` via a deep path (`@/features/payment-methods/presentation/pages/PaymentMethodsPage`) instead of using the feature's barrel export. The barrel (`@/features/payment-methods`) does export `PaymentMethodsPage`.
issues.rule: Route files should import from the feature's public index barrel

issues.description: The `adminRestClient` in `payments` and `adminRest.ts` in `admin/src/app/api/admin/_shared/` both implement `createRestPath`, `validateUuid`, and a Supabase admin REST fetch wrapper. This is duplicated infrastructure.
issues.rule: Shared infrastructure logic used in multiple apps should live in `packages/shared` or a dedicated server package
issues.fix: Extract the shared Supabase admin REST client (UUID regex, createRestPath, validateUuid, adminFetch/adminFetchJson) to `packages/api` or a new `packages/server-utils` package so both apps can import it.

issues.description: 67 feature layers across apps are missing `index.ts` barrel exports (11 domain layers, 21 application layers, 13 infrastructure layers, 22 presentation layers). Without barrel exports, consuming code must use deep paths, which breaks encapsulation and makes refactors harder.
issues.rule: Each feature layer should have index.ts exporting its public API
issues.fix: Add `index.ts` barrel files to each layer that is currently missing one. Prioritize domain and application layers as they are most commonly consumed externally.

issues.description: Inline anonymous type `{ key: string; value: string }` used as `mutationFn` parameter. The `settings` feature already has `domain/types.ts` and the setting keys are typed as `keyof PaymentSettings`. The mutation parameter type would be more expressive as a named type.
issues.rule: Types should be defined in domain/types, not inline in application hooks
issues.fix: Add `export interface UpdateSettingPayload { key: keyof PaymentSettings; value: string; }` to `domain/types.ts` and reference it in the hook.

issues.description: This shared hook is a single re-export of `useAuditLog` from the `audit` feature. A one-line re-export in `shared/` that points back into a specific feature's application layer adds indirection without benefit and obscures the real source.
issues.rule: Shared code must be truly generic and used by 2+ features
issues.fix: Remove `shared/application/hooks/useLogExport.ts` and have consumers import directly from `@/features/audit/application/hooks/useAuditLog` (using the feature barrel if possible).

patterns.description: All route files (page.tsx) consistently follow the thin-wrapper pattern — they import and render a single feature page, passing only locale/id params. Business logic is correctly kept out of the app router.
App URLs are centralized in `packages/shared/src/config/appUrls.ts` using `app-links.json`, driven by environment variables. No hardcoded localhost URLs in production feature code.
Each feature has a top-level `index.ts` barrel export for its public API. Cross-feature imports that do occur use the feature barrel index rather than deep internal paths (except ARCH-004).

Three distinct cross-feature import patterns exist in `apps/studio` (products ↔ orders ↔ seller-admins) and `apps/store` (products ↔ cart). These are pragmatic compositions but violate feature isolation. They may be acceptable given the small scale, but should be tracked.
